danger_signals.visual_form: vehicle sprite changes appearance or stops moving on track
danger_signals.meaning: aging or unreliable vehicle needs maintenance or replacement

danger_signals.visual_form: negative balance display in company finances UI, red text
danger_signals.meaning: company operating at a loss; bankruptcy risk if sustained

danger_signals.visual_form: environmental destruction or vehicle collision animation on map
danger_signals.meaning: random catastrophic event damaging infrastructure or vehicles

danger_signals.visual_form: station rating drops visually in UI, efficiency indicator lowers
danger_signals.meaning: poor service quality; station attracting less cargo

danger_signals.visual_form: rival company vehicles and stations appear in their faction color on isometric map
danger_signals.meaning: competitive threat; rival capturing market share in region

safety_signals.visual_form: positive revenue display in company accounts, green text or upward graph
safety_signals.meaning: transport route successfully moving cargo; steady income

safety_signals.visual_form: station rating UI indicator shows high efficiency, visual highlight
safety_signals.meaning: station is well-serviced and attracts more cargo

safety_signals.visual_form: vehicle sprite continues moving smoothly; no breakdown alerts
safety_signals.meaning: vehicle operating within reliability parameters; minimal maintenance needed

safety_signals.visual_form: loan balance decreases in company finances, interest charges lower
safety_signals.meaning: financial health improving; reduced debt burden

safety_signals.visual_form: new stations, tracks, and vehicles visible on isometric map in player's faction color
safety_signals.meaning: company growing infrastructure; market presence strengthening

faction_indicators.color_identity: player-selected primary color (assigned at game start; customizable per session)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: company name and logo in UI; vehicles and stations display company color
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: rival companies appear in distinct colors; up to 7 AI competitors each with unique faction color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: all player-owned stations, tracks, vehicles, and depots rendered in player's faction color on isometric map

faction_indicators.color_identity: each AI competitor assigned distinct color from palette at game initialization
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: rival company name visible in company league; vehicles/stations show rival color
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: rival vehicles use same sprite base as player vehicles but rendered in rival's color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: rival stations, tracks, vehicles appear scattered across map; color-coded for identification

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: limited technology: only steam trains and horse-drawn road vehicles available; maps show sparse towns and industries; starting loan displayed prominently
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player begins with limited capital; must construct basic rail/road network; vehicles move slowly; economic growth is gradual

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: diesel and electric engines introduced; modern trucks and buses appear; airport infrastructure becomes viable; more towns and industries visible; company balance becomes positive for successful players
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: players expand networks aggressively; competitor presence increases; route optimization becomes critical; vehicle replacement cycles accelerate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: monorail technology appears (1999); modern aircraft and ships visible; established infrastructure dense across map; mature company finances show substantial assets or accumulated debt
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: network consolidation or aggressive expansion; rival companies entrenched; competition for remaining market opportunities; long-term profitability determines survival

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: town growth visible (towns expand on map); increased cargo demand shown in station notifications; high-value goods flowing; general positive financial indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: new routes become profitable; vehicle demand high; expansion feasible; competitors also aggressive; subsidy opportunities increase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: cargo demand decreases; station activity lowers; unprofitable routes show red losses; vehicle prices may adjust; subsidy values reduce if cyclical economy enabled
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: profitability margins shrink; players forced to optimize or consolidate routes; competitor retrenchment occurs; risk of bankruptcy increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: environmental destruction animation on isometric tiles; vehicle crash sprites; infrastructure damage visible as broken/missing stations or tracks
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: affected player loses revenue temporarily; vehicle repair costs spike; competitors may lose vehicles/infrastructure; route disruption forces rerouting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: terraformed tiles show altered height/slope on isometric map; player-constructed canals visible as water paths; leveled ground for station placement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: route optimization possible through environmental modification; construction costs increase; alternative network layouts become viable

environmental_storytelling.example: coal mines visually cluster in certain map regions; steel mills positioned near coal; farms scattered; oil refineries remote; players learn economic geography by observing industry placement and inferring commodity flow

dense town clusters on isometric map indicate high-value passenger markets; sparse towns indicate agricultural regions; town size correlates to revenue potential; player learns priorities by visual inspection

steam engine sprites (early 1930s) visually primitive; diesel engines appear more modern (1950s); electric trains sleek (1970s); monorail futuristic (1999+); vehicle appearance teaches historical timeline

rival company tracks and stations visible on shared map; player observes competitor route choices, station placement, vehicle types; can infer rival strategy and competitive pressure from spatial layout

older vehicles visually degrade or are replaced by newer models; older track technology (rails) eventually supplemented by monorail; visual obsolescence tells player about vehicle/infrastructure lifecycle

successful high-revenue routes visualized by dense vehicle traffic and multiple stations; failing routes show sparse vehicle movement; player learns profitability by observing network activity density

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: moves smoothly along tracks without stopping; no maintenance alerts; sprite renders cleanly
frequent stops for breakdown; sprite may flicker or change appearance; maintenance costs display in UI; eventually must be replaced
continuous movement between stations; carries full cargo; generates revenue per delivery
stops on road network; no cargo movement; repair costs incurred; blocks other vehicles from using road tile
accepts large cargo volumes; rating high; attracts more goods from nearby industries/towns; visual indicator shows efficiency
low cargo acceptance; rating drops; industries avoid sending goods there; visual indicator shows poor efficiency
new stations and vehicles appear frequently in rival color; network grows across map; competitive pressure visible
no new infrastructure visible; existing vehicles remain unchanged; network shrinks; financial distress implied
town growth visible; cargo demand high; route profitability increases; subsidy values higher
cargo demand decreases; station activity lowers; route profitability marginal; subsidy values lower

notes: Transport Tycoon uses isometric 2D graphics and real-time simulation to communicate world-state primarily through visual density, color-coding, and UI numerical indicators. The game lacks explicit narrative or political factions but communicates competitive pressure and economic health through infrastructure visibility and financial displays. Danger and safety signals are primarily quantitative (profit/loss, reliability ratings) made visible through UI elements and behavioral manifestations (vehicle movement, cargo flow). Environmental storytelling relies on industry placement patterns and town distribution to communicate economic geography. Faction identity is player-company-specific, assigned via color selection at game start; rival companies are differentiated only by color, not by thematic or political distinction.
